zh.Heukelom, Noord-Brabant, Nederland: 荷蘭努-布拉班特可洛姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes van der Lecke於1261年出生在荷蘭-拉藍德蒙斯特時，她的父親Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke21歲，母親Jutta van Borssele22歲。她於1283年與Arnoud van der Sluys結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1307年4月1日享年46歲。

zh-hans.Heukelom, Noord-Brabant, Nederland: 荷兰努-布拉班特可洛姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes van der Lecke于1261年出生在荷兰-拉藍德蒙斯特时，她的父亲Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke21岁，母亲Jutta van Borssele22岁。她于1283年与Arnoud van der Sluys结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1307年4月1日享年46岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Lecke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: American shortened form of Flemish and Dutch   Van Hecke  .

variants.name: Heck
meanings: South German: topographic name from Middle High German hecke, hegge ‘hedge’. This surname is common in southern Germany and the Rhineland, and is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).
Dutch: topographic name from Middle Dutch heck ‘fence, gate’. Compare Van Hecke .
English: topographic name for someone who lived by a gate or ‘hatch’ (especially one leading into a forest), northern Middle English heck (Old English hæcce). Compare Hatch .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes van der Lecke was born in 1261, in Monster, South Holland, Netherlands, her father, Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke, was 21 and her mother, Jutta van Borssele, was 22. She married Arnoud van der Sluys in 1283. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 1 April 1307, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes van der Lecke est née en 1261, à Monster, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Jutta van Borssele, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Arnoud van der Sluys en 1283. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 avril 1307, à l’âge de 46 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes van der Lecke 1261 in Monster, Südholland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke 21 und ihre Mutter Jutta van Borssele 22. Sie hat 1283 Arnoud van der Sluys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. April 1307 im Alter von 46 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes van der Lecke è nata il nel 1261 nel Monster, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Jutta van Borssele, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Arnoud van der Sluys nel 1283. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 1 aprile 1307, all'età di 46 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes van der Lecke(이)가 1261년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 몬스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jutta van Borssele(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1283년 Arnoud van der Sluys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1307년 4월 1일 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes van der Lecke родилась в 1261 г. в местности монстир, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Heer Hendrick II van der Lecke было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Jutta van Borssele, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Arnoud van der Sluys в 1283 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 1 апреля 1307 г. в возрасте 46 лет.
